comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Sequencing precondition met 2026-07-14: epic/audit-enrichment landed on main (attest.py rework + manifest.py extraction + plan sidecar lossless v2 + retention 50). remediation_mode_candidate now at src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:182. Story is unblocked and enters the normal review->claim flow.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The rising floor's remediation-mode eligibility (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:182 remediation_mode_candidate) requires a prior CERTIFIED plan-review signature ("signed"). A BLOCK never signs, so the floor is structurally inert in BLOCK loops - the regime driving 76% of extra review rounds. Post-flip field data: 287/382 plan reviews ineligible (240 with signed=False as the first failing precondition), 33 eligible, 0 novelty drops ever. The floor only drops advisories, but BLOCK-loop advisory walls balloon (ticket 36c7: 21 -> 36 advisories over 10 rounds), so engaging it there is real relief.

## What
- Persist the baseline signals in the sidecar: `build_payload(verdict, *, material=None)` in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py gains a `repo_root=None` keyword (its caller `emit` already holds repo_root and passes it through) and stamps two additive top-level fields, ALWAYS self-sourced at emit time with no preferred-source branch and no dependence on verdict contents - so they are present identically on PASS and BLOCK verdicts: `verified_at_sha` = rebar.llm.config.current_code_sha() (no argument - a ContextVar reader of the active gate snapshot) with a git-HEAD fallback when it is None (local-mode reads): the committed HEAD SHA of repo_root, best-effort subprocess, None only if that also fails. The eligibility check resolves the CURRENT side by the same two-step rule, so both sides of code_unchanged are always produced by one resolution and local-mode BLOCK loops (where current_code_sha() is always None) still get a working fallback instead of a structurally inert one and `regver` = the registry version via its repo_root-taking accessor (the same accessor remediation_mode_candidate already uses for its registry_unchanged check; this is what build_payload's new repo_root kwarg is for). Sourcing failures stamp None (never raise; emit stays best-effort). Both fields are additive-optional on the existing plan-review sidecar schema; older sidecars without them simply fail the fallback preconditions (fail-safe).
- Rekey eligibility in remediation_mode_candidate with explicit two-branch control flow: (1) SIGNATURE PATH first, byte-identical to today - a valid certified plan-review manifest supplies material/SHA/regver baselines; (2) SIDECAR FALLBACK only when no valid plan-review manifest exists - baseline = the most recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar; plan_changed compares the current material fingerprint against the sidecar's material_fingerprint; code_unchanged compares the current run's code SHA (current_code_sha(), argument-less) against the sidecar's verified_at_sha - None on either side fails the precondition; registry_unchanged compares the current registry version against the sidecar's regver. A sidecar missing any baseline field leaves that precondition False (never eligible on partial data). BOTH paths that today yield no usable signature baseline - a signature verification error AND a successful verification whose manifest is not a plan-review manifest (the early return at attest.py:231) - now fall through to the sidecar branch instead of returning ineligible; all other fail-safe behavior (any read error -> ineligible -> full review) is preserved. Decision-dict shape per branch: the returned dict gains `baseline: "signature" | "sidecar"`, and `eligible` is computed as all() over THAT branch's reason keys only - the signature branch keeps today's keys verbatim ({signed, plan_changed, code_unchanged, registry_unchanged, prior_sidecar, within_window}); the sidecar branch's reasons dict contains EXACTLY five keys - {sidecar_baseline, plan_changed, code_unchanged, registry_unchanged, within_window} - where `sidecar_baseline` is True when a prior sidecar carrying all three baseline fields (material_fingerprint, verified_at_sha, regver) exists; there is no `signed` and no separate `prior_sidecar` key in that branch (the baseline check subsumes prior-sidecar existence), so eligible = all(reasons.values()) cannot be structurally inert.
- Docs: docs/plan-review-gate.md's eligibility description updated (replacing the honest-limitation paragraph added by task 67da of this epic).

## Scope
IN: two sidecar fields, the two-branch eligibility rekey, docs update, unit tests over the eligibility decision matrix, one integration test over a BLOCK loop. OUT: the drop math (novelty threshold/priority floor unchanged), the novelty scorer itself (task 0730 in this epic), drop permanence semantics, any change to the signature path's semantics when a valid manifest exists.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] With no certified signature but a prior sidecar carrying material_fingerprint + verified_at_sha + regver (plan changed, code + registry unchanged, within window), eligibility is TRUE. Oracle: unit test over remediation_mode_candidate with a fixture sidecar; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "remediation" -x` green.
- [ ] With code drifted since the prior sidecar, OR a prior sidecar missing verified_at_sha or regver, eligibility is FALSE (fail-safe). Oracle: negative-case unit tests in the same module covering both.
- [ ] With a valid certified signature present, the decision is identical to today's signature path (the sidecar fallback is not consulted). Oracle: unit test asserting the signature-path result is unchanged for a signed fixture.
- [ ] A BLOCK-loop integration test (review -> BLOCK -> plan edit -> re-review) reaches the floor path (coverage.remediation.eligible true in the round-2 sidecar) without any signature present. Oracle: `python -m pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_block_loop_remediation.py -x` green, asserting coverage.remediation.eligible is true in the round-2 sidecar payload.
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md's eligibility description matches the new behavior. Oracle (exit-code, machine-readable, against the phrases actually in the doc today): `! grep -q "the floor does not engage during BLOCK loops" docs/plan-review-gate.md` succeeds (the Known-limitation blockquote added by task 67da is removed) AND `grep -qi "sidecar fallback" docs/plan-review-gate.md` succeeds (the replacement paragraph documents the fallback).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all pass.

file_impact.reason: persist review-time code sha if absent

compiled_state.title: Rekey rising-floor eligibility off prior sidecar so BLOCK loops qualify
